title: Cell Line Change with Glycosylation Shift
source: synthetic (SP v5 Section 8.2.1 tricky scenario: CHO DG44 to CHO-K1 cell line change causes coordinated glycosylation micro-heterogeneity shifts that are individually near-tolerance but collectively form a pattern concern)
scenario_description: CHO DG44 to CHO-K1 cell line change. G0F%, afucosylation%, and charge variants all shifted near the edge of tolerance (~2.6-3.6% relative change against 5% physicochemical tolerance). Each attribute individually scores as minor concern, but the coordinated shift pattern across multiple glycosylation-related attributes suggests a fundamental change in the glycosylation machinery. SEC and potency remain comparable. The system should flag glycan attributes for investigation even though the overall verdict is Comparable.
change_description: Cell line change from CHO DG44 to CHO-K1. Glycosylation micro-heterogeneity changed: G0F%, afucosylation%, and charge variants all shifted in the same direction (toward less processed glycoforms). Each attribute is individually near the edge of acceptance tolerance but the pattern of coordinated shifts across multiple glycosylation-related attributes raises concern about fundamentally different post-translational modification. Orthogonal glycan characterization and functional bridging studies are recommended.
_verdict_note: JC verdict is proceed (Comparable) with consistency warning about INVESTIGATE action. Previously overridden to Comparable With Caveats by legacy guard. Known gap: JC confidence (0.91) does not yet penalize action-verdict misalignment.
